name: Registrar Carousel
novelty: Defeats the operator/registrar/ASN fleet-cap by ROTATING the operator dimension instead of stacking it. The fleet-cap is designed to collapse N sources behind ONE registrar/ASN toward one class; it has nothing to say about N domains each behind a DISTINCT registrar+ASN. The class-assignment is offline, so the operator never proves the carousel is one wallet. One actor manufactures K genuinely class-disjoint DOMAIN anchors at $/yr each — priced, but linearly, with no super-linear collapse.
construction: Actor buys K=4..8 registered domains, each through a DIFFERENT registrar (Namecheap, GoDaddy, Porkbun, Cloudflare, Gandi, OVH, Dynadot, Hetzner) and hosts each on a DIFFERENT cloud ASN (AWS, GCP, Azure, DO, Hetzner, Vultr).
Each domain passes DNS-01 honestly -> a real AnchorAttestation with a registrar/ASN-derived operator classId that is DISTINCT per domain.
Because operator classIds differ, the fleet-cap's 'N behind one operator collapse to one class' never fires; the binder emits K distinct DOMAIN independence classes.
All K passports are the same human/wallet, asserting the SAME attribute value V, plus a planted CONTRADICTION value V' on a victim incumbent.
Stagger establishment timestamps >90d apart on a few of them to also clear the high-impact recency-clean window.
expected: Co-asserter naive floods DEFER (prior cycle), but here the K carousel sources are CLASS-DISJOINT, so: (1) MIS over anchor-class disjointness counts the full K independent roots (no shared anchor -> a clique-less-of-shared graph -> max independent set = K, up to MAX_EXACT_ROOTS=18); (2) decisive-or-defer sees K co-asserters of V with no genuine opposition of comparable rep and, once any of them earns rep>=0.20 via mutual corroboration, the gap>=0.30 AND top>=0.20 gate is cleared by the bloc; (3) the high-impact gate's '>=2 disjoint anchor classes' + '>=2 corroborations' is trivially met (K>=4 classes, K-1 corroborations each); (4) AUTO-RESOLVES an irreversible multi-class dispute in the attacker's favor.
mechanism: The fleet-cap caps the WRONG axis: it caps anchor-set OVERLAP, not actor identity. Offline class-assignment cannot see that one wallet funded all K registrations. Independence = anchor-set disjointness is satisfied truthfully while real-world independence is zero. Cost is linear ($40-200/yr total) for an unbounded count gain -- the 'priced not prevented' assumption holds, but the price is far below the trust it buys because reputation and the high-impact gate both treat class count as a proxy for actor count.
downstream_unlocked: count: K (full MIS, no collapse). reputation: each carousel source earns independence-weighted alpha from the OTHER K-1 (mutual corroboration inside the bloc reads as independent corroboration). high-impact gate: cleared (classes>=2, corroborations>=2, recency staggered). disown: even if ONE carousel domain is later disowned, the DERIVATION-BFS + one-hop opt-in corroboration reversal claws back at most that one hop; the other K-1 keep their bloc-earned rep because they carry no DERIVATION edge to the disowned node and the corroboration-event ledger only reverses the exact recorded delta on the directly-intersecting beneficiary.
probes: Bind K=4 DOMAIN anchors with distinct synthetic operator classIds; assert independentRootCount -> expect 4 (proves no fleet-collapse across distinct operators).
Run db.adjudicate(attr, {highImpact:true}) with the K-bloc vs a single fresh incumbent; expect RESOLVED for the bloc (proves the gate counts classes, not actors).
Disown one carousel node; assert the other K-1 retain bloc-earned alpha (proves one-hop reversal leaves the carousel standing).

name: Mega-Provider Subdomain Seam
novelty: Manufactures K DOMAIN classes for ~FREE (not $/yr) by proving DNS-01 control of K subdomains under ONE registrable parent the actor does not even own at eTLD+1 -- github.io, vercel.app, pages.dev, s3.amazonaws.com, web.app. DNS-01 verifies control of the leaf name; the binder derives classId from the proven FQDN, not the Public-Suffix eTLD+1. So a1.github.io, a2.github.io, ... each look like a distinct DOMAIN class while sharing the same operator (GitHub's ASN) AND the same actor.
construction: Actor creates K free pages-style subdomains under a public-suffix provider (each is independently DNS-controllable via the provider's CNAME/TXT mechanism).
Each passes DNS-01 honestly for its FQDN.

If the binder's operator classId is derived from ASN, ALL share GitHub's ASN -> fleet-cap SHOULD collapse them. The seam: many public-suffix providers front their pages on shared/anycast CDN ASNs (Fastly, Cloudflare) DISTINCT from the API ASN, and a TXT-at-leaf prover sees only the leaf -- if operator classId is derived from the registrable name via naive label-stripping (strip one label) rather than the PSL, github.io is treated as the 'registrable' parent for foo.github.io but a1.github.io vs a2.github.io strip to github.io (same) -- collapses. BUT user.github.io is itself eTLD+1 under the PSL's *.github.io private rule: a1.github.io and a2.github.io are SEPARATE registrable names per the PSL private section.

So the correct PSL-aware answer is they ARE separate registrable owners (GitHub intends that); the seam is that the engine has NO PSL and cannot distinguish 'github.io is a public suffix where each leaf is a separate owner' from 'evilcorp.com where each leaf is the SAME owner'. Either choice is wrong for half the cases.
expected: Without a PSL, the binder either (a) treats every FQDN as its own class -> K free DOMAIN classes for one actor under evilcorp.com (sub1.evilcorp.com..subK.evilcorp.com all one owner, counted as K), OR (b) strips one label and collapses *.github.io to one class -> legitimate independent github.io users wrongly fused. The attacker picks whichever the engine does: if (a), use one's-own-domain subdomains for free K-inflation; if (b), the collapse mis-rule is itself a suppression DoS lever against honest pages-hosted sources.
mechanism: Offline class-assignment + no Public Suffix List = the engine cannot compute the registrable boundary, which is the ONLY mechanical definition of 'same DNS owner'. eTLD+1 is exactly the line between 'subdomains I control under my name' (same owner, must collapse) and 'leaves under a public registrar/pages provider' (different owners, must not collapse). Lacking it, DOMAIN-class independence is structurally unsound in both directions.
downstream_unlocked: count: sub1..subK.evilcorp.com counted as K disjoint DOMAIN classes at $9/yr total (one domain). reputation/high-impact/disown: identical bloc dynamics to the registrar carousel but at ~1/Kth the cost -- the cheapest known path to clearing the high-impact gate's >=2-anchor-class requirement.
probes: Bind sub1.evilcorp.com and sub2.evilcorp.com; assert independentRootCount -> if 2, the same-owner seam is open.
Bind a1.github.io and a2.github.io; assert count -> compare against the PSL-correct answer (2 separate owners) to show the engine cannot tell the two scenarios apart.
Feed the K same-parent subdomains into the high-impact gate; expect cleared (proves $9 clears an irreversible decision).

name: Null-Source Laundromat (combined / adaptive E2E)
novelty: Chains the carousel/subdomain class-manufacture with a credit-LAUNDERING stage that survives disown. Bare-key (independence 0, rep_cap 0.05) sources carry NO independence weight and CANNOT be disowned via DERIVATION (they author no derived strands, just raw observed agreement). The actor uses a disposable cloud of bare keys to PUMP corroboration-event credit into ONE real carousel DOMAIN source, then deliberately keeps the credit path corroboration-event-based (agreement) rather than DERIVATION-based -- because disown's reversal is one-hop, opt-in (caller must assert the corroboration event) and DERIVATION-BFS only walks DERIVATION edges.
construction: Stand up the K-class carousel bloc (spec 01) as the 'clean' front.
Stand up M cheap bare-key sources (free, rate-limited only).

Bare keys repeatedly OBSERVE-agree with the front source's claims so the corroboration-event ledger records alpha-mass gains on the front source. Because independence-weighted alpha denies headcount at the caller (one ratify per class) and bare keys are class 'None', each bare key SHOULD contribute ~0 independence weight -- BUT if any bare key is silently promoted to a manufactured DOMAIN class via spec 01/02, its agreement now counts.
Front source's rep climbs on laundered corroboration; it then asserts/contradicts in a high-impact dispute.

If the front is ever disowned, the corroboration reversal must be EXPLICITLY recorded per beneficiary and is one-hop: the laundered gains on SECOND-order beneficiaries (sources that corroborated the front AFTER it was already inflated) are not reversed because the sweep does not transitively re-walk corroboration events.
expected: Two-stage breach: (1) bare-key agreement that should be weightless becomes weighted the instant any laundering key is upgraded to a manufactured class (spec 01/02 supplies the upgrade), so the contradiction-bomb 'fresh = weightless' defense is bypassed by paying the class price once and reusing it; (2) on disown, the DERIVATION-BFS finds no edges (laundromat used agreement, not derivation) and the corroboration-event reversal is one-hop + opt-in, so transitive laundered credit on downstream sources persists -- the 'exact reversal' is exact only for the directly-recorded hop.
mechanism: Three seams compose: (a) class-manufacture turns weightless bare keys into weighted corroborators; (b) disown's reversal is DERIVATION-shaped while the laundering channel is corroboration-shaped, so the BFS misses it; (c) the corroboration-event ledger is opt-in and one-hop, so multi-hop wash trades survive. Each is individually 'acceptable' per the gap list; combined they let an actor inflate, weaponize, and then retain credit through a disown.
downstream_unlocked: count: unchanged (laundromat targets rep, not MIS). reputation: front source reaches rep>=0.20 (decisive gate) and beyond rep_cap-of-bare-key by importing weight from manufactured classes. high-impact: front clears it. disown: PARTIAL clawback only -- first-hop reversed, second-hop laundered credit survives; coincidental-independent protection is (correctly) not punished but is also the cover the laundromat hides behind.
probes: Pump corroboration from M bare keys (class None) into a front source; assert front alpha gain ~0 (baseline), then upgrade one key to a manufactured DOMAIN class and re-pump; assert alpha now rises (proves class-manufacture re-weights laundering).
Disown the front; assert a SECOND-order beneficiary (a source that corroborated the front post-inflation) retains its laundered alpha (proves one-hop reversal gap).
Confirm the DERIVATION-BFS visits 0 nodes for an agreement-only laundromat (proves the channel/reversal shape mismatch).

name: PSL eTLD+1 Collapse at Binding Time
candidate_fix: At DOMAIN-anchor binding, derive the operator/registrable classId from the Public Suffix List eTLD+1 of the proven FQDN (with the PSL PRIVATE section honored, so *.github.io leaves are separate owners but sub1.evilcorp.com / sub2.evilcorp.com collapse to evilcorp.com -> ONE class). Bundle the PSL as a versioned static asset (zero network dep at bind time).
attack_that_proves_gap_today: Spec 02 (Mega-Provider Subdomain Seam): today, with no PSL, sub1..subK.evilcorp.com bind to K distinct DOMAIN classes for one $9 domain, clearing the high-impact gate; and the engine cannot distinguish that from K legitimately-separate github.io owners.
predicted_outcome: PARTIAL. CLOSES the same-owner-subdomain seam (spec 02 collapses to 1 class -> count=1, high-impact gate denied) and correctly separates PSL-private leaves. Cost: bundle ~220KB PSL, a periodic refresh process (stale PSL = a new, slower liability), and a deterministic eTLD+1 function in the binder. FAILS to touch spec 01 (Registrar Carousel): K domains each at a DISTINCT eTLD+1 are genuinely distinct registrable names -- PSL says they are independent because by the DNS trust root they ARE. The PSL fixes the 'same registrable name' lie; it cannot fix the 'same wallet, different registrable names' lie. Residual: the carousel and any cross-domain actor-correlation remain offline-unknowable. Net: necessary hygiene, not a structural close.
cost: Engineering: medium (PSL asset + eTLD+1 parser + PRIVATE-section handling + refresh job). Runtime: negligible. Coverage: spec 02 only.
probes: Post-fix bind a1/a2.github.io -> assert count==2 (PRIVATE section preserved).
Post-fix run spec 01 carousel -> assert count still ==K (proves PARTIAL: registrar carousel untouched).

name: High-Impact Gate Counts Independent ROOTS + Operator-Graph Fleet-Cap
candidate_fix: Two coupled changes: (1) the high-impact gate's '>=2 anchor CLASSES' requirement is replaced/augmented by '>=2 independent ROOTS surviving an actor-correlation discount' -- i.e. count distinct REAL-WORLD operators, not classIds. (2) Extend the fleet-cap from a single registrar/ASN scalar to an OPERATOR-GRAPH cap: cluster anchors by shared payment fingerprint / registrant email / ASN / nameserver-set / TLS-issuer-account and collapse a cluster toward one class even when the registrar string differs.
attack_that_proves_gap_today: Spec 01 (Registrar Carousel): K domains across K registrars/ASNs each get a distinct operator classId; the high-impact gate counts K>=2 anchor classes and clears an irreversible decision for one wallet. Today the fleet-cap only collapses the literally-identical registrar/ASN, which the carousel rotates away from.
predicted_outcome: PARTIAL (tending to FAILS on the hard core). CLOSES the carousel WHEN a shared correlation signal exists and is observable to the operator (same registrant email, same payment processor token, same nameserver host, same ACME account) -- clustering those collapses the bloc to ~1 class and denies both the MIS count and the high-impact gate. FAILS structurally when the attacker buys correlation-FREE: distinct registrant identities, distinct prepaid cards, distinct nameservers, distinct ACME accounts -- which is exactly the 'patient attacker pays a finite cost to mint independent-looking sources' the hard theorem says is unsolvable from inside. The operator-graph cap raises the per-class PRICE (now each carousel domain must also be operationally disjoint) but cannot reach 0 leakage. Counting ROOTS not classes is strictly better (it stops the cheap class-stack) but 'independent root' is still adjudicated from offline class assignment, so it inherits the same blind spot.
cost: Engineering: high (correlation-signal ingestion: WHOIS/RDAP registrant, ASN lookup, nameserver-set, ACME-account fingerprint; a clustering pass; a privacy/PII surface for storing registrant correlators). Operational: the correlation signals are themselves external trust roots that can be spoofed (privacy-proxy WHOIS defeats registrant clustering today). Adversary cost forced up from ~$40 to ~$40+disjoint-operations per class -- a real but finite escalation. Coverage: spec 01 partially, spec 03 stage-1 partially.
probes: Post-fix: carousel with SHARED registrant email -> assert cluster collapses to count<=1 (proves the easy half closes).
Post-fix: carousel with privacy-proxy WHOIS + distinct ACME accounts + distinct nameservers -> assert count still ==K (proves the structural FAILS residual).
Replace gate predicate with root-count; re-run spec 01 -> assert high-impact DENIED when correlation found, CLEARED when correlation-free (quantifies the PARTIAL boundary).

name: Merkle Audit Under Class Forgery (CONFIRM)
candidate_fix: (Probe, not a change) Test whether manufactured/forged independence classes from specs 01-03 can be leveraged to break, rollback, split-view, or equivocate the RFC-6962 Merkle audit chain -- i.e. whether class-assignment forgery propagates into a tamper-evidence break.
attack_that_proves_gap_today: Attempt: use the carousel bloc to (a) get a fraudulent RESOLVED adjudication and then DELETE/REORDER the audit leaves that record the laundering, presenting a consistent-but-false history to one reviewer while showing the honest history to another (split-view).
predicted_outcome: CONFIRM / HOLDS. The Merkle layer's guarantees are ORTHOGONAL to class correctness: domain-separated leaf/node hashes + O(log n) inclusion/consistency proofs + epoch STHs signed by the log key + publication to >=2 independent sinks make deletion, rollback, and split-view DETECTABLE regardless of whether the leaves describe honest or forged claims. A forged-class adjudication is faithfully and immutably recorded; the attacker CANNOT later excise it (a witness's prior STH the live tree cannot extend -> ROLLBACK_OR_DELETION), and cannot show two reviewers divergent histories (consistency-proof mismatch across sinks). The audit layer correctly proves WHAT WAS RECORDED AND IN WHAT ORDER; it makes no claim about whether the recorded classes are real -- and it does not need to. So class forgery is fully AUDITABLE after the fact: every fraudulent resolution is permanently attributable. The ONLY residual is the documented operational gap (the >=2 sinks must be genuinely independent live witnesses; with no live witnesses the guarantee is null) -- which is a deployment item, not a Merkle break.
cost: None (confirmation). Value: the audit layer is the post-hoc accountability backstop for every class-assignment attack above -- it cannot PREVENT a forged resolution but it guarantees the forgery is immutably recorded and the disown sweep / human ratify horn act on an un-rewritable history. Recommendation: pair the audit's immutability with an out-of-band class-audit process, since the chain proves order/inclusion, not class truth.
probes: Drive a forged-class RESOLVED adjudication; capture its audit leaf + STH; attempt to delete the leaf and re-publish -> assert a witness STH non-extension is flagged ROLLBACK_OR_DELETION.
Attempt split-view: publish tree A to sink1, tree B to sink2 -> assert consistency-proof check across sinks fails (equivocation detected).
Flip one byte in the persisted forged-resolution record -> assert verifyChain().ok===false at that seq (semantic tamper named).
